title: AWS MSK Cluster Monitoring
description: Comprehensive monitoring dashboard for Amazon Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka (MSK) clusters. Combines JMX Exporter metrics (via MSK Open Monitoring) with AWS CloudWatch metrics for complete cluster visibility.

var-deployment-environment.name: deployment.environment
var-deployment-environment.queryValue:
SELECT DISTINCT(JSONExtractString(labels, 'deployment.environment')) as `deployment.environment`
FROM signoz_metrics.time_series_v4
WHERE metric_name LIKE '%kafka%'
  AND (unix_milli >= (toUnixTimestamp(now() - toIntervalDay(1)) * 1000))
  AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'deployment.environment') != ''
GROUP BY `deployment.environment`

var-cluster-name.name: cluster.name
var-cluster-name.queryValue:
SELECT DISTINCT(JSONExtractString(labels, 'cluster.name')) as `cluster.name`
FROM signoz_metrics.time_series_v4
WHERE metric_name LIKE '%kafka%'
  AND (unix_milli >= (toUnixTimestamp(now() - toIntervalDay(1)) * 1000))
  AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'cluster.name') != ''
GROUP BY `cluster.name`

var-broker-id.name: broker.id
var-broker-id.queryValue:
SELECT DISTINCT(JSONExtractString(labels, 'kafka.server:type=app-info,id=([^,]+)', 'broker.id')) as `broker.id`
FROM signoz_metrics.time_series_v4
WHERE metric_name LIKE '%kafka%'
  AND (unix_milli >= (toUnixTimestamp(now() - toIntervalDay(1)) * 1000))
  AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'broker.id') != ''
GROUP BY `broker.id`

var-topic.name: topic
var-topic.queryValue:
SELECT DISTINCT(JSONExtractString(labels, 'topic')) as topic
FROM signoz_metrics.time_series_v4
WHERE metric_name LIKE '%kafka.server:type=BrokerTopicMetrics%' 
  AND (unix_milli >= (toUnixTimestamp(now() - toIntervalDay(1)) * 1000))
  AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'topic') != ''
GROUP BY topic

var-consumer-group.name: group
var-consumer-group.queryValue:
SELECT DISTINCT(JSONExtractString(labels, 'group')) as `group`
FROM signoz_metrics.time_series_v4
WHERE metric_name LIKE '%kafka.consumer:type=consumer-fetch-manager-metrics%'
  AND (unix_milli >= (toUnixTimestamp(now() - toIntervalDay(1)) * 1000))
  AND JSONExtractString(labels, 'group') != ''
GROUP BY `group`

widgets.title: Active Controller Count
widgets.description: Number of active controllers in the cluster. Should be exactly 1 for a healthy MSK cluster.

widgets.title: Offline Partitions
widgets.description: Number of partitions that have no leader. Zero is healthy - any non-zero value indicates data availability issues.

widgets.title: Under-Replicated Partitions
widgets.description: Number of partitions where the number of in-sync replicas is less than the replication factor. Zero is healthy.

widgets.title: Leader Count
widgets.description: Number of partitions for which this broker is the leader. Used to verify leader distribution across brokers.

widgets.title: Broker CPU Usage
widgets.description: CPU utilization per broker. High sustained CPU usage may indicate the need for larger instance types.

widgets.title: Broker Memory Usage
widgets.description: Memory consumption per broker in bytes. Monitor for memory pressure that could impact Kafka performance.

widgets.title: Broker Disk Usage
widgets.description: Disk space used by each broker. Monitor disk usage to prevent running out of storage.

widgets.title: Broker Disk Throughput
widgets.description: Read/write throughput on the disk for each broker. High throughput may indicate heavy log I/O.

widgets.title: Messages In Per Second
widgets.description: Number of messages produced to each topic per second. Shows per-topic production rate.

widgets.title: Messages Out Per Second
widgets.description: Number of messages consumed from each topic per second. Shows per-topic consumption rate.

widgets.title: Bytes In Per Second
widgets.description: Volume of data produced to each topic per second. Key metric for understanding data ingestion patterns.

widgets.title: Bytes Out Per Second
widgets.description: Volume of data consumed from each topic per second. Key metric for understanding data consumption patterns.

widgets.title: Produce Failed Rate
widgets.description: Rate of failed produce requests. Non-zero values indicate producers are failing to write messages.

widgets.title: Fetch Failed Rate
widgets.description: Rate of failed fetch requests. Non-zero values indicate consumers are failing to read messages.

widgets.title: Under-Replicated Partitions (Time Series)
widgets.description: Time series view of under-replicated partitions across the cluster. Sustained non-zero values indicate replication lag.

widgets.title: Offline Partitions (Time Series)
widgets.description: Time series view of offline partitions. Any non-zero value means partitions have no leader and data is unavailable.

widgets.title: Partition Count
widgets.description: Total number of partitions across the MSK cluster. Track partition growth for capacity planning.

widgets.title: Partition ISR (In-Sync Replicas)
widgets.description: Number of in-sync replicas per partition. Should match the replication factor for healthy partitions.

widgets.title: Leader Election Rate
widgets.description: Rate of leader elections per second. Frequent elections indicate broker instability.

widgets.title: Consumer Lag
widgets.description: Consumer group lag (max offset lag) showing how far behind consumers are from the latest produced messages.

widgets.title: Consumer Error Rate
widgets.description: Error rate for consumer requests across the MSK cluster. Non-zero values indicate consumer connectivity or processing issues.

widgets.title: Max Offset Lag Per Topic
widgets.description: Maximum offset lag across all partitions per topic. Helps identify which topics have the most consumer lag.

widgets.title: CPU Credit Usage
widgets.description: CPU credit usage for MSK brokers on burstable instance types (T-series). Track credit consumption to avoid CPU throttling.

widgets.title: CPU Credit Balance
widgets.description: Remaining CPU credits for MSK brokers. Low balance indicates the broker is consuming more CPU than its baseline.

widgets.title: Burst Balance
widgets.description: CPU burst balance percentage for MSK brokers. When this reaches 0%, CPU performance is throttled to baseline.

widgets.title: Network I/O
widgets.description: Network input/output for MSK brokers as tracked by AWS CloudWatch. Monitor for network bottlenecks.
